references.text: [At25] C. Atherfold, Almost sure bounds for weighted sums of Rademacher random multiplicative functions. arXiv:2501.11076 (2025).

remarks.text:
This model of a random multiplicative function is sometimes called a Rademacher function, although this is sometimes reserved for a merely multiplicative function (which is $0$ on non-squarefree integers). See [520] for the partial sums of this alternative model.

It should also be compared to another popular model of random completely multiplicative functions, Steinhaus functions, which have $f(p)$ uniformly distributed over the unit circle.

Atherfold \cite{At25} has proved that, almost surely,\[\sum_{m\leq N}f(m)\ll N^{1/2}(\log N)^{1+o(1)}.\]

statement.text: Let $f$ be a random completely multiplicative function, where for each prime $p$ we independently choose $f(p)\in \{-1,1\}$ uniformly at random. Is it true that\[\limsup_{N\to \infty}\frac{\sum_{m\leq N}f(m)}{\sqrt{N}}=\infty\]with probability $1$?
